_meta.description: Theory of change + program portfolio + KPIs + impact measurement. The map of HOW P31 delivers mission. Drives /impact public reporting and grant-application program descriptions.

mission.statement: Build, Create, Connect — establish decentralized family infrastructure that strengthens vulnerable households through privacy-respecting, edge-only technology and lived-experience advocacy.
mission.vision: Every family has the cognitive, technical, and legal scaffolding to weather crisis without surrendering autonomy.
values: Open source by default, copyleft for hub products

theoryOfChange.problemStatement: Vulnerable families (medically complex, neurodivergent, mid-litigation, financially constrained) face compounding friction from siloed services, surveillance-default tech, and credential-based gatekeeping. The result is loss of autonomy at exactly the moment autonomy matters most.
theoryOfChange.intervention: Provide a decentralized, edge-only mesh of tools + documents + cognitive-passport infrastructure that families self-host on free-tier infrastructure, with operator-led advocacy to translate lived experience into policy.
shortTermOutcomes: Families adopt at least one P31 tool (passport, mesh, planetary-onboard)
Operators report reduced cognitive load in crisis episodes
Public artifacts (passport, doc-library) reach external audiences without conversion friction
mediumTermOutcomes: Family mesh becomes default infrastructure for at least 100 households
Open-source ecosystem self-sustains with non-operator contributors
longTermOutcomes: Decentralized-by-default becomes a meaningful alternative to surveillance-tech monopolies for family-scale infrastructure
Lived-experience operators are recognized as legitimate technical authorities in their domains
Replicable model exists for other operator-led nonprofits in adjacent fields
assumptions: Free-tier edge infrastructure remains viable at modest scale
Open-source contribution norms remain healthy enough to sustain non-operator development
Privacy-respecting tech retains regulatory protection (e.g. encryption is not banned)
risks: Free-tier policy changes that force budget infrastructure
Mission drift toward saleable enterprise SaaS

programs.name: Cognitive Passport
programs.shortDescription: Identity-first cognitive context document that travels with an individual across providers, employers, schools, courts.
programs.primaryBeneficiary: individuals with neurodivergence, chronic illness, or complex needs requiring repeated context-handoffs

outcomeKpis.kpi: operator-reported friction reduction in 30-day post-passport survey

programs.name: K₄ Family Mesh
programs.shortDescription: Edge-only encrypted family communication and state-sync infrastructure on Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects.
programs.primaryBeneficiary: vulnerable families (medical, legal, neurodivergent) needing coordination without surveillance

outcomeKpis.kpi: median operator spoon-cost per crisis episode (self-reported)
data sovereignty audit pass-rate (no plaintext at rest at any vertex)

programs.name: Open Document Library
programs.shortDescription: Searchable static markdown index of P31 canon — design, governance, philosophy, advocacy. Public, CC-BY licensed.
programs.primaryBeneficiary: researchers, journalists, allied organizations, AI/agent crawlers

programs.name: Operator-Led Advocacy
programs.shortDescription: Translation of lived-experience operations into policy artifacts — court filings, accommodation guidance, replicable advocacy templates.
programs.primaryBeneficiary: self-represented litigants with disabilities or executive-function constraints

outcomeKpis.kpi: downstream operators citing P31 templates in court filings

programs.name: Household Youth Vibe-Coding
programs.shortDescription: Local-first technical apprenticeship for the operator's children and similarly-positioned youth — building real systems on real infrastructure.

impactMeasurement.framework: Outcomes-over-outputs; lived-experience attestation prioritized over external validation
quantitativeMethods: Tool-adoption metrics (privacy-respecting: aggregate counts only, no individual tracking)
Search visibility (curl + open APIs, not surveillance analytics)

annualImpactReport.deliverable: Markdown + PDF, published at /impact/ within 6 months of fiscal year end

evaluationCadence.perProgramQuarterly: internal — staff/operator review of each program's KPIs
evaluationCadence.boardAnnual: board reviews program portfolio + considers wind-down/expansion
evaluationCadence.externalEvaluation: when annual revenue exceeds $500k OR per major-funder requirement
